vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file permission bits on /etc/gshadow, a standard STIG hardening step. Adjusting permissions to 0000 does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services and cannot lock out SSH or console access.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Incorrect existing permissions may allow unauthorized group password reads; Applying permission change without verifying current ownership could cause temporary audit warnings
